seed_notes: Before first run, apply the separate profile patch sheet to seed_base + 0x40 through the debugger.
Set MSP from the first word in the image and PC from the second word after patching.
For each row, add all bytes and keep the low 8 bits for S. XOR all bytes for X.
Verify each row before entry, then verify HASH32 after reconstructing the full image.
Patch table lives at seed_base + 0x40. The embedded seed defaults keep UART disabled until patched.
After the patch sheet is applied, the monitor words are: ?, I, P, ., @, !, C@, C!, L, X, B.
patch_notes: These writes are applied after the generic seed is loaded and before the seed is started.
Write the values below to seed_base + patch_table_offset using SWD, JTAG, or another debugger/programmer path.
Read back the written words to verify them, then set MSP and PC from the seed vector table and resume execution.
Patch flags bit 0 enables UART, bit 1 enables RX, bit 2 enables TX, bit 3 selects debugger-only mode, bit 4 marks shared status semantics.
Example post-patch monitor flow: 20001000 10 L then 16 bytes of hex data, then 20001000 B.
